Summary: [INFRA-01] Project scaffolding and Docker Compose setup
Description:
As a developer, I want a fully containerised local dev environment so that the full stack can be started with a single command.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Docker Compose file starts backend (FastAPI/Uvicorn), frontend (React), and PostgreSQL 15+ services.
2. Backend is reachable at http://localhost:8000/api/v1.
3. Frontend is served at http://localhost:3000.
4. Database initialises with an empty schema on first run.
5. All environment-specific values (DB credentials, JWT secret, gateway keys) are loaded from a .env file — no secrets in source code.

As a developer, I want versioned database migrations so that schema changes are tracked and reproducible.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Alembic is configured with asyncpg async driver.
2. An initial migration creates all core tables (users, members, trainers, subscription_types, subscriptions, payments, facilities, classes, reservations, trainer_availability, password_reset_tokens).
3. Running alembic upgrade head on a clean DB applies all migrations without errors.
4. Direct DDL changes are prohibited; all schema changes must go through migration scripts.

As a developer, I want an automated pipeline so that tests run on every push and the build is validated.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Pipeline runs on every push/PR to main.
2. Pipeline stages: lint (ruff/flake8 + ESLint), unit tests, integration tests, Docker build.
3. Pipeline fails and blocks merge if any stage fails.
4. Test coverage report is generated and published as a pipeline artifact.

As an operator, I want structured JSON logs so that log aggregation tools can parse and query them.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Every log entry is valid JSON containing: timestamp, log_level, service_name, request_id, message.
2. No PII (email, phone) appears in logs in plaintext.
3. Log level is configurable via environment variable.

As a developer, I want auto-generated API docs so that frontend and QA can explore endpoints without reading source code.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. FastAPI auto-generates OpenAPI 3.x docs available at /api/v1/docs.
2. Every endpoint documents request body, path/query params, and response schemas.
3. Auth-protected endpoints are marked with the Bearer security scheme.

As a user, I want to log in with email and password so that I can access my role-appropriate dashboard.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-1) Login form accepts email and password.
2. (REQ-2) System validates credentials against stored bcrypt hash.
3. (REQ-3) On success, returns access token (15-min TTL) and refresh token (7-day TTL) in response body.
4. (REQ-4) User is redirected to their role-appropriate dashboard (Admin / Member / Trainer).
5. Invalid credentials return a generic error that does not reveal which field is wrong.

As a user, I want to reset my password via email so that I can regain access if I forget it.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-5) Reset email is sent within 30 seconds of the request.
2. (REQ-6) Reset token expires 60 minutes after issuance.
3. (REQ-7) Each token is single-use; a second click returns an error.
4. (REQ-8) New password requires at least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, and one digit.
5. The email input form returns a generic success message regardless of whether the email exists (no user enumeration).

As a frontend client, I want to silently refresh my access token so that the user session continues uninterrupted after token expiry.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-9) POST /api/v1/auth/refresh accepts a valid refresh token and returns a new access token.
2. (REQ-10) Revoked refresh tokens (e.g., after password change) are rejected with 401.
3. (REQ-11) Refresh tokens are rotated on each use and stored securely.
4. Frontend automatically redirects to login on 401 from the refresh endpoint.

As a system, I want every API endpoint to enforce role-based permissions so that users cannot access resources outside their role.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-12) Access control is enforced server-side on every protected endpoint; client-side hiding is supplementary only.
2. (REQ-13) Administrators have access to all resources.
3. (REQ-14) Members can only access their own profile, subscription, payment history, and reservations.
4. (REQ-15) Trainers can only access their own profile, schedule, and reservation lists of their own classes.
5. Requests with insufficient role return HTTP 403 Forbidden.

As a security control, I want accounts to be temporarily locked after repeated failed login attempts so that brute-force attacks are mitigated.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-16) Account is locked after 5 consecutive failed login attempts.
2. (REQ-17) Lockout duration is 15 minutes; account unlocks automatically.
3. (REQ-18) A notification email is sent to the account owner when lockout is triggered.
4. (REQ-19) Administrators can manually unlock any account from the admin panel.
5. A locked user sees a clear lockout message with remaining time.

As a security control, I want inactive frontend sessions to expire automatically so that unattended sessions cannot be exploited.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-20) Inactivity timeout is 30 minutes, configurable by an Administrator.
2. (REQ-21) A warning dialog is shown 60 seconds before session expiry.
3. (REQ-22) On expiry, all locally stored tokens are cleared and user is redirected to login.
4. Any user interaction resets the inactivity timer.

As an administrator, I want to register new members so that they can access the system.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-23) Creation form captures: first name, last name, email, phone, date of birth.
2. (REQ-24) Email uniqueness is validated before record creation.
3. (REQ-25) A welcome email with a password-set link is sent to the new member.
4. (REQ-26) Newly created member defaults to 'inactive' subscription status.
5. Duplicate email submission shows a validation error without creating the record.

As a user, I want to edit a member profile so that personal details remain accurate.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-27) Members may edit: first name, last name, phone number, and password.
2. (REQ-28) Administrators may edit all fields including subscription status and role.
3. (REQ-29) Invalid data returns field-level error messages before persistence.
4. A member cannot edit another member's profile (returns 403).

As a user, I want to view a member profile so that I can see relevant details.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-30) Profile view shows: personal details, subscription status, active subscription summary, and registration date.
2. (REQ-31) Administrators additionally see: payment history link, reservation history link, and account status (active/restricted/locked).
3. A member attempting to view another member's profile receives 403 Forbidden.

As an administrator, I want to deactivate member accounts so that former members can no longer access the system.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-32) Deactivation sets the user's is_active flag to false.
2. (REQ-33) Future reservations of a deactivated member are automatically cancelled with a notification email.
3. (REQ-34) Administrators can reactivate a previously deactivated account.
4. A deactivated member attempting to log in sees an 'account inactive' message.
5. A confirmation modal is shown before deactivation listing the impact.

As an administrator, I want to search the member database so that I can quickly locate a specific member.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-35) Search queries across: first name, last name, email address, and phone number.
2. (REQ-36) Search is case-insensitive and supports partial matches.
3. (REQ-37) Results are displayed in a paginated list with 20 items per page by default.
4. Search is debounced (live search as user types).
5. 'No members found' message is shown for empty results.

As an administrator, I want to filter the member list by status so that I can manage specific cohorts efficiently.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-38) Filter supports statuses: Active, Expired, Restricted, Inactive.
2. (REQ-39) Multiple filters can be active simultaneously.
3. (REQ-40) Active filter state is reflected in URL query parameters (bookmarkable).
4. Clearing filters restores the unfiltered list.

As an administrator, I want to export the member list to CSV so that I can use it in external tools.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-41) Exported CSV includes: first name, last name, email, phone, subscription status, registration date.
2. (REQ-42) Export reflects any active search or filter criteria.
3. (REQ-43) Filename includes the export date (e.g., members_2025-03-15.csv).
4. Export triggered on a filtered list includes only filtered members.

As an administrator, I want a dedicated view of restricted members so that I can act on expired subscriptions efficiently.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-44) A member is restricted when their subscription end_date is past and status has not been renewed.
2. (REQ-45) Restricted members appear in a dedicated list, separate from the active member list.
3. (REQ-46) List shows: member name, email, subscription end date, days since expiry.
4. Selecting a restricted member offers options to renew subscription or deactivate account.

As an administrator, I want to create a new subscription for a member so that they gain active access to the fitness center.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-47) Subscription is linked to exactly one member and one subscription type.
2. (REQ-48) End date is automatically calculated from the type's duration in days.
3. (REQ-49) A member may not have two overlapping active subscriptions.
4. (REQ-50) Creating a subscription updates the member's subscription_status to 'active'.
5. A pricing preview (dates and total amount) is shown before confirmation.

As a user, I want to view the full details of a subscription so that I understand my current plan and pricing.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-51) Detail view shows: subscription type, start date, end date, base fee, PT sessions (if personalized), total amount, and current status.
2. (REQ-52) For personalized subscriptions, the pricing formula is displayed: Base Fee + (PT Sessions x 50 RON).
3. A member accessing another member's subscription URL receives 403 Forbidden.

As an administrator, I want to renew a member's subscription so that they regain active status without a gap.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-53) Renewal creates a new subscription record without modifying the existing record.
2. (REQ-54) New subscription start date defaults to the day after the current subscription's end date.
3. (REQ-55) Member status is updated to 'active' upon renewal even if it was 'restricted'.
4. Renewal form is pre-populated with the same subscription type.

As an administrator, I want to cancel an active subscription so that a member's access is revoked.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-56) Cancellation sets subscription status to 'cancelled' and records the cancellation date.
2. (REQ-57) Cancellation updates the member's subscription_status to 'restricted'.
3. (REQ-58) Future reservations under the cancelled subscription are automatically cancelled.
4. A confirmation modal displays the impact (number of future reservations affected) before cancellation.

As an administrator, I want to configure a personalized subscription with a variable number of PT sessions so that pricing reflects individual training plans.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-59) Pricing formula: Total = Base Fee + (Number of PT Sessions x 50 RON).
2. (REQ-60) PT session count must be a positive integer.
3. (REQ-61) System stores base fee, PT session count, and computed total separately to support receipt generation.
4. Selecting the 'Personalized' subscription type reveals a PT sessions input field.
5. Total is live-calculated as the administrator enters PT session count.

As the system, I want to automatically expire subscriptions past their end date so that administrators do not need to manually manage status transitions.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-62) A background scheduled job runs daily at midnight.
2. (REQ-63) Active subscriptions with end_date before the current date are set to 'expired'.
3. (REQ-64) The associated member is automatically set to 'restricted' status.
4. (REQ-65) An expiry notification email is sent to the member on the day their subscription expires.
5. Job execution and any errors are logged in structured JSON.

As an administrator, I want to define and manage the catalog of subscription types so that they are available for assignment to members.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-66) Each subscription type has: a name, duration in days, and base fee in RON.
2. (REQ-67) A subscription type in use by at least one active subscription cannot be deleted; it can only be archived.
3. (REQ-68) Archived types do not appear in the new subscription form but remain readable for historical records.
4. Subscription type names must be unique.

As a member, I want to pay for my subscription online by card so that I can activate my membership digitally.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-69) Card data is collected exclusively through the gateway's certified JS SDK or hosted payment page.
2. (REQ-70) System stores the gateway payment reference ID but never stores card numbers, CVVs, or expiry dates.
3. (REQ-71) Payment status is updated via webhook, not based on client-side redirect alone.
4. (REQ-72) System handles payment statuses: pending, succeeded, failed, refunded.
5. Webhook signatures are verified using HMAC-SHA256.
6. Failed payments update payment status and notify the member.

As a user, I want to view a chronological list of all payments so that I have a complete transaction record.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-73) Each payment entry shows: payment date, subscription type, amount, currency, and status.
2. (REQ-74) List is sorted by date descending by default.
3. (REQ-75) Members see only their own payment history; administrators can view any member's history.
4. Clicking a transaction shows full details and a link to the receipt.
5. List is paginated.

As a member, I want to receive and download a payment receipt so that I have proof of payment.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-76) Receipt includes: receipt number, date, member name, subscription type, pricing breakdown, total amount, and gateway reference.
2. (REQ-77) Receipts are downloadable as PDF files.
3. (REQ-78) A receipt copy is emailed to the member within 2 minutes of payment confirmation.
4. Members can download receipts on demand from their payment history.

As an administrator, I want to generate a monthly revenue report so that I can track financial performance.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-79) Report aggregates: total revenue for selected month, revenue by subscription type, and number of payments.
2. (REQ-80) Report is filterable by month and year.
3. (REQ-81) Report is exportable as CSV.
4. Refunded payments are excluded from revenue totals (per REQ-84).

As an administrator, I want to log manual refund requests so that refunds are tracked even when processed outside the system.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-82) Refund record stores: original payment ID, refund amount, reason, refund date, and the admin who logged it.
2. (REQ-83) A payment may only be marked as refunded once.
3. (REQ-84) Refunded payments are excluded from revenue report totals.
4. Refund is logged via a form accessible from the payment detail page.

As an administrator, I want to create training class sessions so that members can discover and reserve spots.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-85) Class record must contain: name, description, trainer (FK), facility (FK), start time, end time, and capacity.
2. (REQ-86) System prevents creating a class in a facility if another class occupies an overlapping time slot in that facility.
3. (REQ-87) System prevents assigning a trainer who already has a class at an overlapping time.
4. Missing required fields highlight the field and prevent form submission.
5. Class is immediately visible to members for reservation after creation.

As an administrator, I want to edit training class details so that corrections and updates are reflected for members.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-88) New capacity value cannot be less than the current number of active reservations.
2. (REQ-89) Changing start or end time triggers email notifications to all members with active reservations.
3. (REQ-90) Editing does not affect the status of existing reservations.
4. Conflict checks (facility and trainer) are re-evaluated on save.

As an administrator, I want to cancel a training class so that all reserved members are notified and spots are released.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-91) Cancellation sets class status to 'cancelled'.
2. (REQ-92) All active reservations for the class are set to 'cancelled'.
3. (REQ-93) Each affected member receives an email notification within 5 minutes of class cancellation.
4. Confirmation modal lists the number of affected reservations before cancellation.

As a user, I want to view the upcoming class schedule so that I can plan my training.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-94) Schedule displays classes for at least the next 4 weeks.
2. (REQ-95) Each class entry shows: name, trainer, facility, start time, end time, and available spots out of total capacity.
3. (REQ-96) Past classes are hidden in the default view; accessible via a date range filter.
4. (REQ-97) Trainer view highlights only their assigned classes.
5. Toggle between weekly calendar and list view is supported.

As the system, I want to enforce the maximum participant limit atomically so that classes are never over-booked.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-98) Spot check and decrement are performed atomically to prevent race conditions under concurrent requests.
2. (REQ-99) A class is considered full when active reservations equal the capacity field.
3. (REQ-100) Available spot counts are updated in real time; the schedule view must not show stale counts.
4. A full-class reservation attempt returns a 'class is full' message and offers the waitlist.

As an administrator, I want to assign or reassign a trainer to a class so that the schedule is always staffed correctly.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-101) System checks for scheduling conflicts before confirming a trainer assignment.
2. (REQ-102) A trainer may not be assigned to two classes with overlapping time slots.
3. (REQ-103) Reassigning a trainer sends a notification to both the old and new trainer.
4. Deactivated trainers do not appear in the trainer selection dropdown.

As an active member, I want to reserve a spot in an upcoming class so that I am guaranteed a place.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-104) Only members with 'active' subscription status may make reservations.
2. (REQ-105) A member may not have more than one active reservation for the same class.
3. (REQ-106) Spot reservation is atomic to prevent double-booking under concurrent requests.
4. (REQ-107) A confirmation notification is sent immediately after a successful reservation.
5. Restricted or inactive members see a message explaining their status.

As a user, I want to cancel a reservation so that the spot is released for other members.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-108) Cancellation sets reservation status to 'cancelled' and records the cancellation timestamp.
2. (REQ-109) Cancellations within 2 hours of class start time are flagged as late cancellations.
3. (REQ-110) On cancellation, the system checks the waitlist and offers the spot to the next waitlisted member.
4. A confirmation step is shown before cancellation is processed.
5. Administrators can cancel any reservation; members can only cancel their own.

As a member, I want to view all my reservations so that I can track upcoming and past classes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-111) Reservation list is split into two sections: Upcoming and Past.
2. (REQ-112) Each entry shows: class name, trainer, facility, date and time, and reservation status.
3. (REQ-113) Upcoming reservations include a Cancel button if cancellation is still permitted.
4. Clicking a reservation shows full class details.

Summary: [RES-04] View all reservations for a class
Description:
As an administrator or trainer, I want to view the full participant list for a class so that I can prepare and manage attendance.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-114) Participant list shows: member name, email, reservation date, and reservation status.
2. (REQ-115) List is downloadable as CSV by administrators.
3. (REQ-116) Total participant count and remaining capacity are displayed at the top.
4. Trainer view includes attendance tracking controls.

As a member, I want to join the waitlist for a full class so that I am notified if a spot becomes available.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-117) Waitlist is ordered strictly by the time the member joined.
2. (REQ-118) When promoted from the waitlist, the member receives an email and/or SMS with a 2-hour confirmation window.
3. (REQ-119) If the promoted member does not confirm within 2 hours, the spot is offered to the next waitlisted member.
4. (REQ-120) Members can remove themselves from a waitlist.
5. Waitlist offer is triggered immediately when an active reservation is cancelled.

As a member, I want to receive a confirmation notification immediately after reserving a class so that I have all details in one place.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-121) Confirmation notification is sent within 1 minute of the reservation being created.
2. (REQ-122) Email includes: class name, trainer name, facility name, date, start time, end time, and a cancellation link.
3. (REQ-123) Notification is sent via email; SMS is optional if member has opted in with a phone on file.
4. Delivery failures are logged and retried up to 3 times.

As a member, I want to receive a reminder before my class so that I do not forget to attend.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-124) Reminder is sent 24 hours before class start time.
2. (REQ-125) Reminder includes: class name, trainer, facility, start time, and a cancellation link.
3. (REQ-126) Members who have already cancelled do not receive a reminder.
4. (REQ-127) Members can opt out of reminder notifications from profile settings.
5. Scheduler runs hourly and identifies reservations starting in approximately 24 hours.

As an administrator, I want to register new trainers so that they can be assigned to classes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-128) Creation form captures: first name, last name, email, phone, and specializations.
2. (REQ-129) A welcome email with a password-set link is sent to the trainer on account creation.
3. (REQ-130) New trainer account is assigned the 'trainer' role by default.
4. Duplicate email returns a validation error without creating the record.

As a user, I want to edit a trainer profile so that profile information stays current.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-131) Trainers may edit: phone number, bio/description, and profile photo.
2. (REQ-132) Administrators may edit all fields including specializations and active status.
3. A trainer attempting to edit another trainer's profile receives 403 Forbidden.
4. Changes are validated before persistence with field-level error messages.

As an administrator, I want to deactivate a trainer account so that they can no longer log in or be assigned to classes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-133) Deactivation sets the trainer's is_active flag to false.
2. (REQ-134) Upcoming classes assigned to the deactivated trainer are flagged with 'trainer missing' status and appear in an admin alert dashboard.
3. (REQ-135) Deactivated trainers do not appear in the trainer selection dropdown when creating or editing a class.
4. Confirmation modal lists upcoming affected classes before deactivation.

As a trainer or administrator, I want to view a trainer's upcoming class schedule so that workload and planning can be managed.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-136) Schedule shows: class name, facility, start time, end time, and current participant count.
2. (REQ-137) Schedule is viewable as a list (default) or a weekly calendar.
3. (REQ-138) Schedule covers at least the next 30 days.
4. Trainers can only view their own schedule; administrators can view any trainer's schedule.

As a trainer, I want to define my recurring weekly availability so that administrators can schedule me during suitable hours.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-139) Availability is stored as a recurring weekly schedule (day of week + time range).
2. (REQ-140) Availability is advisory only and does not prevent class assignment outside available hours.
3. (REQ-141) The availability grid covers the full 7-day week in 30-minute intervals.
4. A soft warning is shown when assigning a trainer to a class outside their stated availability.

As an administrator, I want to define facilities so that they can be assigned to training classes.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-142) Facility record contains: name, description, and maximum capacity (persons).
2. (REQ-143) Facility names must be unique within the system.
3. (REQ-144) Newly created facilities are immediately available in the class creation dropdown.

As an administrator, I want to edit facility details so that information stays accurate as the center evolves.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-145) System prevents saving a new capacity value if any future scheduled class for that facility has more reservations than the proposed new capacity.
2. (REQ-146) Changes to the facility name are reflected everywhere the facility is referenced in the UI.
3. A warning is shown when reducing capacity that would affect scheduled classes.

As an administrator, I want to generate a facility utilisation report so that I can make data-driven decisions about scheduling.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-147) Report shows per facility: total scheduled class hours, total available participant slots, total reservations made, and occupancy rate (reservations / slots as a percentage).
2. (REQ-148) Report is filterable by date range and by individual facility.
3. (REQ-149) Report is exportable as CSV.

As an administrator, I want to view a calendar of facility bookings so that I can avoid scheduling conflicts at a glance.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-150) Calendar displays all facilities side by side for the selected week.
2. (REQ-151) Each booked block displays: class name and trainer name.
3. (REQ-152) Free (unbooked) slots are visually distinct from booked slots.
4. Clicking a booking block shows class detail.
5. Navigation to the next or previous week loads the corresponding schedule.

As an administrator, I want to generate a member activity report so that I can identify engaged and inactive members.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-153) Report shows per member: total reservations made, total cancellations, and late cancellation count within the selected period.
2. (REQ-154) Report is sortable by any column.
3. (REQ-155) Report is filterable by date range and by subscription status.
4. (REQ-156) Report is exportable as CSV.

As the system, I want to proactively notify members when their subscription is approaching expiry so that they have time to renew before being restricted.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. (REQ-157) Expiry reminder emails are sent at 7 days and again at 1 day before the subscription end date.
2. (REQ-158) Reminder email includes: expiry date, subscription type name, and a direct link to the renewal/payment page.
3. (REQ-159) If a member renews before the reminder is due, the reminder is not sent.
4. (REQ-160) Members can opt out of expiry reminders from profile settings.
5. Scheduler runs daily and identifies matching subscriptions.

As a security-conscious team, I want the application to enforce security controls so that it is protected against common attacks.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Passwords are stored as bcrypt hashes with minimum cost factor 12.
2. JWT tokens are signed using RS256 or HS256 with a secret of at least 256 bits.
3. All API endpoints validate and sanitise inputs via SQLAlchemy ORM parameterisation (no raw SQL).
4. Rate limiting is applied to auth endpoints: max 10 requests per IP per minute.
5. All traffic uses HTTPS with TLS 1.2+. HTTP redirects to HTTPS.
6. A GDPR data deletion endpoint allows administrators to fully remove a member's personal data.
7. Sensitive config values are loaded from environment variables only.

As a developer, I want a comprehensive test suite so that regressions are caught before they reach production.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Unit tests cover all service-layer business logic functions independently of the HTTP framework.
2. Integration tests use httpx AsyncClient to test all API endpoints against a real test database.
3. Aggregate test coverage is at or above 70% for both backend and frontend.
4. Tests run successfully in the CI pipeline.
5. pytest-asyncio is used for async test support.
